renderer.h(3) Library Functions Manual renderer.h(3)

NAME

renderer.h - Terminal-to-pixel renderer and render-file output (audio + video).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct term_renderer_config_t

Typedefs


typedef struct terminal_renderer_s terminal_renderer_t
typedef struct render_file_ctx_s render_file_ctx_t

Enumerations


enum term_renderer_theme_t { TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK = 0, TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT = 1, TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO = 2 }
Terminal rendering theme.

Functions


asciichat_error_t term_renderer_create (const term_renderer_config_t *cfg, terminal_renderer_t **out)
asciichat_error_t term_renderer_feed (terminal_renderer_t *r, const char *ansi_frame, size_t len)
const uint8_t * term_renderer_pixels (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_width_px (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_height_px (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_pitch (terminal_renderer_t *r)
void term_renderer_destroy (terminal_renderer_t *r)
asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char *output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t **out)
void render_file_set_audio_source (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, void *audio_media_source, void *audio_capture_rb)
asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, const char *ansi_frame, uint64_t captured_ns)
void render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t render_file_destroy (render_file_ctx_t *ctx)

Detailed Description

Terminal-to-pixel renderer and render-file output (audio + video).

Supports theme-aware rendering that adapts colors based on the terminal's background theme (dark or light) for optimal readability.

Definition in file renderer.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct render_file_ctx_s render_file_ctx_t

Definition at line 54 of file renderer.h.

typedef struct terminal_renderer_s terminal_renderer_t

Definition at line 17 of file renderer.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum term_renderer_theme_t

Terminal rendering theme. Determines color palette for pixel-based ANSI text rendering. When TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO is used, theme is detected from terminal_has_dark_background() to adapt colors to user's theme.

Enumerator

TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK
Dark theme: use light colors for dark background
TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT
Light theme: use dark colors for light background
TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO
Auto-detect theme from terminal (default)

Definition at line 26 of file renderer.h.

26              {
27   TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK = 0,  
28   TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT = 1, 
29   TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO = 2,  
30 } term_renderer_theme_t;










Function
  Documentation




asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char *
  output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t
  ** out)


Definition at line 28 of file renderer.c.


29                                                               {
30   log_info("[RENDER_FILE_CREATE] CALLED with output_path=%s, cols=%d, rows=%d, fps=%d, theme=%d, out=%p",
31            output_path ? output_path : "(null)", cols, rows, fps, theme, (void *)out);
32   render_file_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*ctx), render_file_ctx_t *);
33 
34   // Resolve font spec → platform-appropriate file path, family name, or bundled data.
35   char font_spec[512] = {0};
36   bool font_is_path = false;
37   const uint8_t *font_data = NULL;
38   size_t font_data_size = 0;
39   const char *raw_font = GET_OPTION(render_font);
40 
41   // Log the font option value from GET_OPTION (for WASM font debugging)
42   log_info("[WASM_FONT_GETTER] GET_OPTION(render_font) returned: '%s' (empty=%d), GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)=%d",
43            raw_font ? raw_font : "(null)", (!raw_font || raw_font[0] == '\0') ? 1 : 0, GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
44 
45   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set, unless user explicitly overrides with --render-font
46   log_debug("raw_font=%s, matrix_rain=%d", raw_font, GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
47   if ((!raw_font || raw_font[0] == '\0') && GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
48     raw_font = "matrix";
49     log_debug("render_file_create: [MATRIX] Using matrix font (enabled by --matrix flag)");
50   }
51 
52   asciichat_error_t fe =
53       platform_font_resolve(raw_font, font_spec, sizeof(font_spec), &font_is_path, &font_data, &font_data_size);
54   if (fe != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
55     log_warn("renderer: font resolution failed for '%s' — using system default",
56              raw_font ? raw_font : "(explicit system)");
57   }
58 
59   log_debug("render_file_create: Font resolved: font_spec='%s', font_is_path=%d, font_data=%p (size=%zu)", font_spec,
60             font_is_path, (void *)font_data, font_data_size);
61 
62   // Use reasonable default font size for render-file quality
63   double font_size_pt = GET_OPTION(render_font_size);
64 
65   term_renderer_config_t tr_cfg = {
66       .cols = cols,
67       .rows = rows,
68       .font_size_pt = font_size_pt,
69       .theme = (term_renderer_theme_t)theme,
70       .font_is_path = font_is_path,
71       .font_data = font_data,
72       .font_data_size = font_data_size,
73   };
74   SAFE_STRNCPY(tr_cfg.font_spec, font_spec, sizeof(tr_cfg.font_spec));
75 
76   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_create(&tr_cfg, &ctx->renderer);
77   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
78     log_error("render_file_create: term_renderer_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
79     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
80     return err;
81   }
82   int actual_width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
83   int actual_height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
84   log_info("render_file_create: GRID DIMENSIONS: %ux%u cells -> PIXEL DIMENSIONS: %dx%d px", cols, rows,
85            actual_width_px, actual_height_px);
86   log_debug("render_file_create: term_renderer created (%dx%d cells, %dx%d px)", cols, rows, actual_width_px,
87             actual_height_px);
88 
89   err = ffmpeg_encoder_create(output_path, actual_width_px, actual_height_px, fps, &ctx->encoder);
90   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
91     log_error("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
92     term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);
93     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
94     return err;
95   }
96   log_debug("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder created successfully");
97 
98   // Initialize audio fields
99   ctx->audio_sample_rate = 48000; // Audio pipeline is 48kHz
100   // Allocate large buffer for snapshot mode (5 seconds at 48kHz with ~14 FPS = 3,428 samples per frame)
101   // Use 8192 to comfortably handle snapshot reads without excessive overhead
102   ctx->audio_buf_size = 8192; // Temporary buffer for reading audio (in floats)
103   ctx->audio_read_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->audio_buf_size * sizeof(float), float *);
104   ctx->audio_media_source = NULL;
105   ctx->audio_capture_rb = NULL;
106 
107   log_info("renderer: initialized encoder for %s", output_path);
108 
109   // Register render file context for debugging
110   NAMED_REGISTER_CONTEXT(ctx, "render_file_ctx", output_path, NULL);
111 
112   *out = ctx;
113   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
114 }

327     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
328   asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(ctx->encoder);
329   term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);

asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame
  (render_file_ctx_t * ctx, const char * ansi_frame, uint64_t
  captured_ns)


Definition at line 124 of file renderer.c.


124                                                                                                                 {
125   // Write to debug file directly to bypass logging system
126 #if !defined(NDEBUG) && !defined(_WIN32)
127   FILE *dbg = fopen("/tmp/render-debug.txt", "a");
128   if (dbg) {
129     fprintf(dbg, "[RENDER_WRITE_FRAME] Called with ctx=%p, frame_len=%zu, captured_ns=%llu\n", (void *)ctx,
130             ansi_frame ? strlen(ansi_frame) : 0, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
131     fclose(dbg);
132   }
133 #endif
134   log_info("render_file_write_frame: CALLED - ctx=%p, captured_ns=%llu", (void *)ctx, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
135 
136   if (!ctx) {
137     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ctx is NULL");
138     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
139   }
140 
141   if (!ansi_frame) {
142     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ansi_frame is NULL");
143     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
144   }
145 
146   size_t frame_len = strlen(ansi_frame);
147   log_info("render_file_write_frame: processing frame (len=%zu)", frame_len);
148   if (frame_len > 0) {
149     log_info("  first 100 chars: %.100s", ansi_frame);
150   }
151 
152   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_feed(ctx->renderer, ansi_frame, frame_len);
153   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
154     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: term_renderer_feed failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
155     return err;
156   }
157 
158   const uint8_t *pixels = term_renderer_pixels(ctx->renderer);
159   int pitch = term_renderer_pitch(ctx->renderer);
160   int width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
161   int height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
162 
163   log_info("render_file_write_frame: pixels=%p pitch=%d dims=%dx%d", (void *)pixels, pitch, width_px, height_px);
164 
165   // Check pixels in multiple locations to verify content
166   if (pixels) {
167     // The framebuffer is RGBA (4 bytes per pixel)
168     // Check pixel at (0,0) - top left corner
169     uint8_t sample_r = pixels[0], sample_g = pixels[1], sample_b = pixels[2], sample_a = pixels[3];
170 
171     // Check pixel at (500, 100) where we'll inject a test red stripe
172     size_t test_offset = 100 * pitch + 500 * 4; // 4 bytes per pixel for RGBA
173     uint8_t test_r = pixels[test_offset], test_g = pixels[test_offset + 1], test_b = pixels[test_offset + 2],
174             test_a = pixels[test_offset + 3];
175 
176     // Check pixel at middle of screen
177     size_t mid_offset = (height_px / 2) * pitch;
178     uint8_t sample_r_mid = pixels[mid_offset], sample_g_mid = pixels[mid_offset + 1],
179             sample_b_mid = pixels[mid_offset + 2], sample_a_mid = pixels[mid_offset + 3];
180 
181     log_info("  pixel[0,0]: RGBA(%u,%u,%u,%u), test_pos[500,100]: RGBA(%u,%u,%u,%u), pixel[0,%d]: RGBA(%u,%u,%u,%u)",
182              sample_r, sample_g, sample_b, sample_a, test_r, test_g, test_b, test_a, height_px / 2, sample_r_mid,
183              sample_g_mid, sample_b_mid, sample_a_mid);
184   }
185 
186   // CRITICAL: Copy pixel buffer before encoding
187   // term_renderer_pixels() returns a pointer to the renderer's internal buffer which gets
188   // overwritten on the next frame. We must copy the data before passing to the encoder.
189   uint8_t *pixels_copy = NULL;
190   if (pixels) {
191     size_t pixel_buffer_size = (size_t)pitch * (size_t)height_px;
192     pixels_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(pixel_buffer_size, uint8_t *);
193     memcpy(pixels_copy, pixels, pixel_buffer_size);
194     log_info("render_file_write_frame: copied %zu bytes of pixel data (was at %p, now at %p)", pixel_buffer_size,
195              (void *)pixels, (void *)pixels_copy);
196   }
197 
198   err = ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(ctx->encoder, pixels_copy, pitch, captured_ns);
199   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
200     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
201   }
202 
203   // Free the pixel copy (ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame reads and converts it immediately)
204   SAFE_FREE(pixels_copy);
205 
206   // Write synchronized audio for this frame if available
207   // In snapshot mode, track actual FPS and calculate samples_per_frame dynamically
208   if (ctx && ctx->audio_read_buf && ctx->encoder) {
209     // Detect if we're in snapshot mode
210     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
211     double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
212 
213     int samples_per_frame;
214 
215     // Track frame timing for dynamic FPS calculation in snapshot mode
216     static uint64_t first_frame_ns = 0;
217     static int log_once = 0;
218 
219     if (first_frame_ns == 0) {
220       first_frame_ns = captured_ns;
221     }
222 
223     if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
224       // In snapshot mode, write samples based on actual elapsed wall-clock time
225       // This will be overridden by the time-based calculation below
226       samples_per_frame = (int)(ctx->audio_sample_rate / 60.0);
227 
228       // Ensure minimum to avoid rounding to 0
229       if (samples_per_frame < 100) {
230         samples_per_frame = 100;
231       }
232 
233     } else {
234       // Normal/live mode: use 60 FPS baseline
235       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_sample_rate / 60;
236       if (!log_once++) {
237         log_info("[AUDIO_CALC] Normal mode: samples_per_frame=%d", samples_per_frame);
238       }
239     }
240 
241     if (samples_per_frame > ctx->audio_buf_size) {
242       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_buf_size;
243       if (!log_once++) {
244         log_warn("[AUDIO_CALC] samples_per_frame clamped to buffer size %d", ctx->audio_buf_size);
245       }
246     }
247     if (samples_per_frame <= 0) {
248       samples_per_frame = 800; // Fallback minimum
249     }
250 
251     int samples_read = 0;
252     static int audio_eof_reached = 0;
253     static int total_audio_samples_written = 0;
254 
255     // In snapshot mode, write samples based on elapsed wall-clock time to match snapshot_delay exactly
256     int actual_samples_per_frame = samples_per_frame;
257     if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
258       uint64_t elapsed_ns = captured_ns - first_frame_ns;
259       double elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / 1e9;
260       double target_fraction = elapsed_sec / snapshot_delay;
261       target_fraction = (target_fraction > 1.0) ? 1.0 : target_fraction; // Cap at 100%
262 
263       int total_target_samples = (int)(ctx->audio_sample_rate * snapshot_delay);
264       int expected_samples_at_this_point = (int)(target_fraction * total_target_samples);
265 
266       // Calculate how many samples to write for THIS frame
267       actual_samples_per_frame = expected_samples_at_this_point - total_audio_samples_written;
268 
269       // Never write more than the buffer allows
270       if (actual_samples_per_frame > ctx->audio_buf_size) {
271         actual_samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_buf_size;
272       }
273       // Never write negative or zero (shouldn't happen, but safety check)
274       if (actual_samples_per_frame <= 0) {
275         actual_samples_per_frame = 0;
276       }
277     }
278 
279     // Read from whichever audio source is available (prefer media source, fallback to capture)
280     if (!audio_eof_reached && ctx->audio_media_source) {
281       // Read from media source (file/URL audio) - try to get samples
282       samples_read = media_source_read_audio(ctx->audio_media_source, ctx->audio_read_buf, actual_samples_per_frame);
283 
284       if (samples_read <= 0) {
285         // EOF reached or error - no more audio samples available from source
286         audio_eof_reached = 1;
287         samples_read = 0;
288       }
289     } else if (!audio_eof_reached && ctx->audio_capture_rb) {
290       // Read from ring buffer (live mic capture)
291       samples_read = audio_ring_buffer_read(ctx->audio_capture_rb, ctx->audio_read_buf, actual_samples_per_frame);
292       if (samples_read < 0) {
293         samples_read = 0; // On error, write silence
294         audio_eof_reached = 1;
295       } else if (samples_read == 0) {
296         audio_eof_reached = 1;
297       }
298       log_debug("render_file_write_frame: reading audio from capture_rb (samples_per_frame=%d, read=%d)",
299                 actual_samples_per_frame, samples_read);
300     }
301 
302     // Write audio: either from source or silence to fill remaining time
303     if (samples_read > 0) {
304       // We got samples from the source - write them
305       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_read);
306       total_audio_samples_written += samples_read;
307     } else if (actual_samples_per_frame > 0) {
308       // No samples available (EOF reached or not available) - write silence to maintain sync
309       // This ensures audio track duration matches video track duration
310       memset(ctx->audio_read_buf, 0, actual_samples_per_frame * sizeof(float));
311       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, actual_samples_per_frame);
312       total_audio_samples_written += actual_samples_per_frame;
313     }
314   }
315 
316   return err;
317 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_buf_size,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_capture_rb,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_media_source,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_read_buf, audio_ring_buffer_read(),
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_sample_rate,
    render_file_ctx_s::encoder, ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(),
    ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(), GET_OPTION, log_debug,
    log_info, log_once, log_warn,
    media_source_read_audio(), render_file_ctx_s::renderer,

    term_renderer_height_px(), term_renderer_pitch(),

Referenced by session_display_encode_frame().






asciichat_error_t term_renderer_create (const
  term_renderer_config_t * cfg, terminal_renderer_t ** out)


Definition at line 195 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


195                                                                                                      {
196   terminal_renderer_t *r = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*r), terminal_renderer_t *);
197   r->cols = cfg->cols;
198   r->rows = cfg->rows;
199   r->theme = cfg->theme;
200 
201   if (FT_Init_FreeType(&r->ft_lib)) {
202     SAFE_FREE(r);
203     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "FreeType init failed");
204   }
205 
206   if (cfg->font_data && cfg->font_data_size > 0) {
207     if (FT_New_Memory_Face(r->ft_lib, cfg->font_data, (FT_Long)cfg->font_data_size, 0, &r->ft_face)) {
208       FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
209       SAFE_FREE(r);
210       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "FreeType: cannot load bundled font");
211     }
212   } else {
213     if (FT_New_Face(r->ft_lib, cfg->font_spec, 0, &r->ft_face)) {
214       FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
215       SAFE_FREE(r);
216       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "FreeType: cannot load font '%s'", cfg->font_spec);
217     }
218   }
219 
220   // Detect if this is the matrix font (needs character mapping to Private Use Area)
221   r->is_matrix_font = (strstr(cfg->font_spec, "matrix") != NULL || strstr(cfg->font_spec, "Matrix") != NULL) ||
222                       (cfg->font_data != NULL && GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
223   if (r->is_matrix_font) {
224     log_debug("term_renderer_create: Detected matrix font - will use Private Use Area character mapping");
225   }
226 
227   // Handle scalable vs bitmap fonts differently
228   // Bitmap fonts (like matrix) don't respond to FT_Set_Char_Size
229   // Instead, use FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes to request a specific pixel size
230   if (r->ft_face->num_fixed_sizes > 0) {
231     // Bitmap font: use FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes to select appropriate size (12px is reasonable for ASCII)
232     int err = FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes(r->ft_face, 12, 12);
233     log_debug("term_renderer_create: [BITMAP] FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes(12, 12) returned %d", err);
234 
235     // If that fails, try FT_Select_Size as fallback
236     if (err != 0) {
237       err = FT_Select_Size(r->ft_face, 0); // Use first available strike
238       log_debug("term_renderer_create: [BITMAP FALLBACK] FT_Select_Size(0) returned %d", err);
239     }
240   } else {
241     // Scalable font: use FT_Set_Char_Size
242     // FT_Set_Char_Size takes 1/64pt units and DPI — supports fractional point sizes.
243     // 96 DPI is the standard screen DPI used here; the 64 factor is the 26.6 fixed-point scale.
244     int err = FT_Set_Char_Size(r->ft_face, 0, (FT_F26Dot6)(cfg->font_size_pt * 64.0), 96, 96);
245     log_debug("term_renderer_create: [SCALABLE] FT_Set_Char_Size(size_pt=%.1f → %ld 1/64pt) returned %d",
246               cfg->font_size_pt, (long)(cfg->font_size_pt * 64.0), err);
247   }
248 
249   // For matrix fonts, try PUA glyph first (U+E900) since ASCII chars might not have bitmap data
250   // For other fonts, start with 'M'
251   int load_err;
252   if (r->is_matrix_font) {
253     log_debug("Matrix font detected: trying PUA glyph U+E900 first");
254     load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 0xE900, FT_LOAD_RENDER);
255     log_debug("FT_Load_Char(U+E900) returned %d", load_err);
256 
257     // If PUA glyph fails, try ASCII fallback
258     if (load_err != 0) {
259       log_debug("U+E900 failed, trying fallback character 'M'");
260       load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'M', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
261       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('M') returned %d", load_err);
262     }
263   } else {
264     // For non-matrix fonts, standard approach
265     load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'M', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
266     log_debug("DEBUG: FT_Load_Char('M', FT_LOAD_RENDER) returned %d", load_err);
267 
268     // For bitmap fonts (like matrix), FT_Load_Char may fail if the character doesn't exist.
269     // Try a fallback character if 'M' fails.
270     if (load_err != 0 && r->ft_face->num_fixed_sizes > 0) {
271       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('M') failed for bitmap font, trying fallback character 'A'");
272       load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'A', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
273       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('A') returned %d", load_err);
274 
275       if (load_err != 0) {
276         // Last resort: try a PUA glyph for unknown bitmap fonts
277         log_debug("FT_Load_Char('A') also failed, trying PUA glyph U+E900");
278         load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 0xE900, FT_LOAD_RENDER);
279         log_debug("FT_Load_Char(U+E900) returned %d", load_err);
280       }
281     }
282   }
283 
284   // For monospace ASCII grid: use advance.x (proper character spacing) and rendered height
285   FT_Pos advance_x_26_6 = r->ft_face->glyph->advance.x;
286   log_debug("DEBUG: advance.x (26.6pt)=%ld", advance_x_26_6);
287 
288   // Calculate cell width from font metrics
289   r->cell_w = (int)(advance_x_26_6 >> 6);
290 
291   // Safeguard: for bitmap fonts, cell_w might be wrong if the font metrics are off
292   // Ensure we have a reasonable minimum width
293   if (r->cell_w <= 0) {
294     log_warn("term_renderer_create: cell_w is %d (invalid), using default 10px", r->cell_w);
295     r->cell_w = 10;
296   }
297 
298   // Only add spacing to fill entire frame if --stretch is specified
299   bool stretch_mode = GET_OPTION(stretch);
300   if (stretch_mode) {
301     r->cell_w++;
302     log_debug("ADVANCE_X: value=%ld (26.6pt) → cell_w=%d (advance + 1 for stretch), cols=%d → width_px=%d",
303               advance_x_26_6, r->cell_w, r->cols, r->cols * r->cell_w);
304   } else {
305     log_debug("ADVANCE_X: value=%ld (26.6pt) → cell_w=%d (preserve aspect ratio), cols=%d → width_px=%d",
306               advance_x_26_6, r->cell_w, r->cols, r->cols * r->cell_w);
307   }
308 
309   // For both bitmap and scalable fonts, use the actual glyph bitmap height
310   // This ensures text doesn't overflow cells. Using size->metrics.height (line spacing)
311   // instead of bitmap.rows causes cells to be too large and text to overflow.
312   r->cell_h = r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap.rows;
313   int raw_bitmap_top = r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap_top;
314   log_debug("DEBUG: cell_h=%d (from bitmap.rows), raw_bitmap_top=%d", r->cell_h, raw_bitmap_top);
315 
316   // Fallback: if cell_h is 0 (bitmap failed to load), use aspect ratio calculation
317   if (r->cell_h <= 0) {
318     log_warn("term_renderer_create: cell_h is 0 (FT_Load_Char failed or no bitmap), using aspect ratio fallback");
319     r->cell_h = r->cell_w * 2;
320     log_debug("FALLBACK: cell_h set to %d (2x width=%d)", r->cell_h, r->cell_w);
321   }
322 
323   // Apply aspect ratio correction unless --stretch is specified
324   // Terminal characters are typically 2:1 (height:width) to appear normal
325   // BUT: only correct if the loaded glyph height is reasonably close (±30%)
326   // This prevents huge jumps from small glyphs (e.g., 8px → 32px)
327   if (!stretch_mode && r->cell_h > 0) {
328     int corrected_h = r->cell_w * 2;
329     int min_acceptable = (corrected_h * 70) / 100;  // 70% of target
330     int max_acceptable = (corrected_h * 130) / 100; // 130% of target
331 
332     if (r->cell_h < min_acceptable || r->cell_h > max_acceptable) {
333       // Loaded height is far from expected, apply correction
334       log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Correcting cell_h from %d to %d (out of range [%d,%d], 2x width=%d)",
335                 r->cell_h, corrected_h, min_acceptable, max_acceptable, r->cell_w);
336       r->cell_h = corrected_h;
337     } else if (corrected_h != r->cell_h) {
338       // Loaded height is close to expected, fine-tune by small amount only
339       int delta = corrected_h - r->cell_h;
340       if (delta > 4 || delta < -4) {
341         // Only apply if difference is more than 4px
342         log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Fine-tuning cell_h from %d to %d (within acceptable range, 2x width=%d)",
343                   r->cell_h, corrected_h, r->cell_w);
344         r->cell_h = corrected_h;
345       } else {
346         log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Keeping cell_h=%d (close to target %d, delta=%d)", r->cell_h, corrected_h, delta);
347       }
348     }
349   }
350 
351   // Position baseline vertically within the cell.
352   // Baseline should be positioned at approximately 75% of cell height to allow proper
353   // glyph rendering with space both above and below. This prevents glyphs from being
354   // clipped at the cell boundaries.
355   //
356   // Baseline position: y_glyph = py + r->baseline - g->bitmap_top
357   // We want: 0 <= baseline - bitmap_top < cell_h (glyph fits within cell)
358   // Choose: baseline = (3 * cell_h) / 4 (positions at 75% height)
359   r->baseline = (3 * r->cell_h) / 4;
360   log_debug("DEBUG: baseline set to %d (75%% of cell_h=%d)", r->baseline, r->cell_h);
361 
362   r->width_px = r->cols * r->cell_w;
363   r->height_px = r->rows * r->cell_h;
364   log_debug("DEBUG: calculated dimensions: width_px=%d (cols=%d * cell_w=%d), height_px=%d (rows=%d * cell_h=%d)",
365             r->width_px, r->cols, r->cell_w, r->height_px, r->rows, r->cell_h);
366   // Pitch in bytes: each pixel is 4 bytes (RGBA), already aligned to 4-byte boundary
367   int base_pitch = r->width_px * 4;
368   r->pitch = base_pitch;
369   log_debug("PITCH_CALC: width_px=%d, base_pitch=%d, final_pitch=%d (RGBA, no padding needed)", r->width_px, base_pitch,
370             r->pitch);
371   log_debug("DEBUG: Allocating framebuffer: %d bytes total (%d pitch * %d height)",
372             (int)((size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px), r->pitch, r->height_px);
373   r->framebuffer = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px, uint8_t *);
374 
375   r->vt = vterm_new(r->rows, r->cols);
376   r->vts = vterm_obtain_screen(r->vt);
377 
378   // Enable UTF-8 encoding so vterm correctly parses multi-byte characters
379   vterm_set_utf8(r->vt, 1);
380 
381   // Set size BEFORE reset to ensure screen is allocated with correct dimensions
382   vterm_set_size(r->vt, r->rows, r->cols);
383   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_set_size called with rows=%d cols=%d", r->rows, r->cols);
384 
385   vterm_screen_set_callbacks(r->vts, &g_vterm_cbs, r);
386   // Reset MUST come before any input writing to initialize state correctly
387   vterm_screen_reset(r->vts, 1);
388 
389   // Verify the size was set correctly after reset
390   int vt_rows, vt_cols;
391   vterm_get_size(r->vt, &vt_rows, &vt_cols);
392   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_get_size returned rows=%d cols=%d (expected %d,%d)", vt_rows, vt_cols, r->rows, r->cols);
393 
394   // CRITICAL: Set size AGAIN after reset, as reset may have affected it
395   vterm_set_size(r->vt, r->rows, r->cols);
396   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_set_size called AGAIN after reset");
397 
398   // Initialize per-renderer glyph cache (empty, will populate on demand)
399   r->glyph_cache = NULL;
400 
401   *out = r;
402   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
403 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    terminal_renderer_s::baseline, terminal_renderer_s::cell_h,
    terminal_renderer_s::cell_w, term_renderer_config_t::cols,
    terminal_renderer_s::cols, ERROR_INIT, ERROR_NOT_FOUND,
    term_renderer_config_t::font_data,
    term_renderer_config_t::font_data_size,
    term_renderer_config_t::font_size_pt,
    term_renderer_config_t::font_spec,
    terminal_renderer_s::framebuffer,
    terminal_renderer_s::ft_face, terminal_renderer_s::ft_lib,
    GET_OPTION, terminal_renderer_s::glyph_cache,
    terminal_renderer_s::height_px,
    terminal_renderer_s::is_matrix_font, log_debug,
    log_warn, terminal_renderer_s::pitch,
    term_renderer_config_t::rows, terminal_renderer_s::rows,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, SET_ERRNO,
    term_renderer_config_t::theme, terminal_renderer_s::theme,
    terminal_renderer_s::vt, terminal_renderer_s::vts, and
    terminal_renderer_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_create().






void
  term_renderer_destroy (terminal_renderer_t * r)


Definition at line 556 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


556                                                    {
557   if (!r)
558     return;
559   vterm_free(r->vt);
560   FT_Done_Face(r->ft_face);
561   FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
562   SAFE_FREE(r->framebuffer);
563   glyph_cache_destroy(&r->glyph_cache);
564   SAFE_FREE(r);
565 }






References terminal_renderer_s::framebuffer,
    terminal_renderer_s::ft_face, terminal_renderer_s::ft_lib,
    terminal_renderer_s::glyph_cache, SAFE_FREE, and
    terminal_renderer_s::vt.


Referenced by render_file_create(), and
    render_file_destroy().






asciichat_error_t term_renderer_feed
  (terminal_renderer_t * r, const char * ansi_frame, size_t len)


Definition at line 405 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


405                                                                                                  {
406   // Clear framebuffer to ensure no leftover pixels from previous frames
407   uint8_t def_bg = (r->theme == TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT) ? 255 : 0;
408   memset(r->framebuffer, def_bg, (size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px);
409 
410   // Reset scrolling region and cursor position to prevent vertical shifts
411   static const char reset_sequence[] = "\033[2J\033[H\033[r";
412   vterm_input_write(r->vt, reset_sequence, sizeof(reset_sequence) - 1);
413 
414   // Count newlines to determine CRLF conversion size
415   int newline_count = 0;
416   for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
417     if (ansi_frame[i] == '\n')
418       newline_count++;
419   }
420 
421   // Convert LF-only line endings to CRLF (vterm expects CRLF)
422   // This fixes cursor positioning on alternate rows
423   char *fixed_frame = SAFE_MALLOC(len + newline_count, char *);
424   if (!fixed_frame) {
425     log_error("failed to allocate memory for CRLF conversion");
426     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "CRLF conversion allocation failed");
427   }
428 
429   size_t fixed_pos = 0;
430   for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
431     fixed_frame[fixed_pos++] = ansi_frame[i];
432     if (ansi_frame[i] == '\n' && (i == 0 || ansi_frame[i - 1] != '\r')) {
433       // Insert carriage return before LF if not already preceded by CR
434       fixed_frame[fixed_pos - 1] = '\r';
435       fixed_frame[fixed_pos++] = '\n';
436     }
437   }
438 
439   vterm_input_write(r->vt, fixed_frame, fixed_pos);
440   SAFE_FREE(fixed_frame);
441 
442   // Calculate centering offsets to position grid in center of canvas
443   int grid_width_px = r->cols * r->cell_w;
444   int grid_height_px = r->rows * r->cell_h;
445   int offset_x = (r->width_px - grid_width_px) / 2;
446   int offset_y = (r->height_px - grid_height_px) / 2;
447 
448   static int frame_count = 0;
449   static char prev_row_sigs[52][5] = {0}; // Store signatures of previous frame's rows
450   frame_count++;
451 
452   // Log if any row signature changed to detect vertical shifts
453   if (frame_count % 10 == 0) {
454     char curr_row_sigs[52][5];
455     for (int row = 0; row < r->rows && row < 52; row++) {
456       // Get first 4 chars as signature
457       for (int col = 0; col < 4 && col < r->cols; col++) {
458         VTermScreenCell cell;
459         vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){row, col}, &cell);
460         curr_row_sigs[row][col] = (cell.chars[0] >= 32 && cell.chars[0] <= 126) ? cell.chars[0] : '.';
461       }
462       curr_row_sigs[row][4] = 0;
463     }
464     memcpy(prev_row_sigs, curr_row_sigs, sizeof(curr_row_sigs));
465   }
466 
467   int glyph_rendered_count = 0;
468   for (int row = 0; row < r->rows; row++) {
469     for (int col = 0; col < r->cols; col++) {
470       VTermScreenCell cell;
471       vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){row, col}, &cell);
472 
473       uint8_t fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb;
474       if (VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.fg)) {
475         fr = cell.fg.rgb.red;
476         fg = cell.fg.rgb.green;
477         fb = cell.fg.rgb.blue;
478       } else {
479         fr = fg = fb = 204;
480       }
481 
482       if (VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.bg)) {
483         br = cell.bg.rgb.red;
484         bg = cell.bg.rgb.green;
485         bb = cell.bg.rgb.blue;
486       } else {
487         br = bg = bb = def_bg;
488       }
489 
490       int px = offset_x + col * r->cell_w, py = offset_y + row * r->cell_h;
491       for (int dy = 0; dy < r->cell_h; dy++) {
492         int y = py + dy;
493         if (y < 0 || y >= r->height_px)
494           continue;
495         uint8_t *line = r->framebuffer + y * r->pitch + px * 4;
496         for (int dx = 0; dx < r->cell_w; dx++) {
497           int x = px + dx;
498           if (x < 0 || x >= r->width_px)
499             continue;
500           line[dx * 4] = br;
501           line[dx * 4 + 1] = bg;
502           line[dx * 4 + 2] = bb;
503           line[dx * 4 + 3] = 255;
504         }
505       }
506 
507       if (cell.chars[0] && cell.chars[0] != ' ') {
508         // For matrix font, map ASCII characters to Private Use Area glyphs (U+E900-U+E91A)
509         uint32_t char_to_render = r->is_matrix_font ? matrix_char_map(cell.chars[0]) : cell.chars[0];
510 
511         // Use per-renderer glyph cache to avoid rasterizing every glyph every frame
512         FT_Bitmap *cached_bitmap = glyph_cache_get(r, r->ft_face, char_to_render);
513         if (cached_bitmap && cached_bitmap->width > 0 && cached_bitmap->rows > 0) {
514           // Find cache entry to get positioning offsets
515           glyph_cache_entry_t *cache_entry = NULL;
516           HASH_FIND_INT(r->glyph_cache, &char_to_render, cache_entry);
517           if (cache_entry) {
518             blit_glyph(r, cached_bitmap, px + cache_entry->bitmap_left, py + r->baseline - cache_entry->bitmap_top,
519                         fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb);
520             glyph_rendered_count++;
521           }
522         }
523       }
524     }
525   }
526   log_info("term_renderer_feed: Rendered %d glyphs out of %d cells", glyph_rendered_count, r->rows * r->cols);
527 
528   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
529 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    terminal_renderer_s::baseline,
    glyph_cache_entry_t::bitmap_left,
    glyph_cache_entry_t::bitmap_top, terminal_renderer_s::cell_h,
    terminal_renderer_s::cell_w, terminal_renderer_s::cols,
    ERROR_MEMORY, terminal_renderer_s::framebuffer,
    terminal_renderer_s::ft_face,
    terminal_renderer_s::glyph_cache,
    terminal_renderer_s::height_px,
    terminal_renderer_s::is_matrix_font, log_error,
    log_info, terminal_renderer_s::pitch,
    terminal_renderer_s::rows, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT,
    terminal_renderer_s::theme, terminal_renderer_s::vt,
    terminal_renderer_s::vts, and
  terminal_renderer_s::width_px.

  uint8_t * term_renderer_pixels (terminal_renderer_t *
  r)
